articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council asserts that implementing licensing fees for landlords won't result in increased rent costs for tenants.  
- Landlords face a £694 per property fee, which the council acknowledges they may not be able to recoup.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls

articles.title: Should landlords be more flexible on tenancy length?

articles.title: Shock survey says another 5% of private rental stock could go
articles.summary: A specialist mortgage lender has surveyed the private rental sector

- The potential reduction in rental stock highlights ongoing challenges in the private rental sector, potentially exacerbating the housing crisis in the UK.

articles.title: Tenant and children put at risk by rogue landlord

articles.title: BLOG: Will AI eat your job in estate agency? No – and here’s why
articles.summary:
The fast-developing tech behind Artificial Intelligence will soon make many procedural jobs obsolete, but what those within the property industry?
The post BLOG: Will AI eat your job in estate agency? No – and here’s why appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- While AI may automate procedural tasks, it won't replace the essential human touch and expertise needed in estate agency.  
- The property industry will adapt and thrive by leveraging AI technology to enhance, not eliminate, job roles.
